title: Los Angeles Dodgers vs. New York Yankees: Here's How to Watch the MLB Game Live Online
description:
If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission.

	After the
body:
If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission.

	After the MLB All-Star Week, the two of the biggest markets go head-to-head to compete for bragging right of the best city in the league. We’d also see which is the better league, the National League or American League.

	On Sunday, July 19, and the second game of a doubleheader, the Los Angeles Dodgers takes on the New York Yankees at Yankee Stadium in The Bronx, New York.

	With L.A. competing against New York, it’s likely the game may features a slew of celebrities in attendance to support their favorite teams, such as Dodgers-fans Rob Lowe, Will Ferrell, Hailey Bieber and others, and Yankees-fans Spike Lee, Jay-Z, Jennifer Lopez and more.


	Meanwhile, it’s a prime time game on a major broadcast network, so it’s likely that it’s going to be a big ratings draw.
	
		When Is Los Angeles Dodgers vs. New York Yankees 2026? Date, Time	

	Broadcasting on NBC, first pitch of the game is at 7:20 p.m. ET/4:20 p.m. PT. However, NBC Sports‘ coverage starts at 7 p.m. ET/4 p.m. PT.
	
		How to Watch Los Angeles Dodgers vs. New York Yankees Online	

	The Dodgers-Yankees is available to stream on Sling TV online, while other way sto watch NBC without cable is through web-based cable alternative services, such as DirecTV, Hulu + Live TV, Peacock and others.


						
			
	

					Sling TV
	
					

	Sling is one of the best options for affordable live streaming is with the Sling Orange + Blue package, which goes for $60.99/month afterwards. The package includes NBC, as well as more than 50 live channels, and 50 hours of cloud DVR to record programming. Please note: Pricing and channel availability are subject to your local TV market.



						
			
	

					DirecTV
	
					

	DirecTV offers NBC with their “Entertainment” package and above with prices starting at $59.99 for the first month ($89.99/month afterward), while the service includes more than 90 channels — such as ABC, CBS, FOX, ESPN, FOX Sports, TNT and others. The streamer has a free 5-day trial available, which is more than enough time to watch online for free. You can either cancel or keep the service after the free trial is over.


						
			
	

					NBCUniversal
	
					

	The Dodgers-Yankees livestream is accessible on Peacock, so the best way to watch the game is to purchase a subscription starting at $10.99/month here. Not a subscriber? You can get a Peacock monthly subscription to stream L.A. Dodgers vs. New York Yankees online, and keep it to watch other hits — including “Obsession,” “The Wild Robot,” “How To Train Your Dragon,” “Reminders of Him,” “The Traitors,” “Vanderpump Rules” and others.


						
			
	

					Fubo
	
					

	At $63.99 for the first month of service ($88.99/month afterwards), Fubo is one of the best options for streaming the Dodgers-Yankees game. The service features NBC, as well as more than 220 live TV channels for cable-free streaming and an unlimited cloud DVR. Watch the game with Fubo’s 5-day free trial. You can even stream on up to two devices at the same time.


						
			
	

					Hulu
	
					

	Starting at $89.99/month, Hulu + Live TV is a great option for live channel streaming access to NBC, along with more than 100 other channels, as well as access to hit originals on Disney+, Hulu and ESPN Unlimited.

schema.headline: Los Angeles Dodgers vs. New York Yankees: Here's How to Watch the MLB Game Live Online

title: Anne Hathaway Says ‘Princess Diaries 3’ Had a ‘Story Breakthrough’ After Having to ‘Start Over’ With the Script: ‘We are Moving in the Right Direction’
description: “Princess Diaries 3” recently had a breakthrough after a scrapped script, Anne Hathaway revealed during an appearance on SiriusXM’s “Radio Andy.”
body:
“Princess Diaries 3” recently had a breakthrough after a scrapped script, Anne Hathaway revealed during an appearance on SiriusXM’s “Radio Andy.” 

	“I’m busy making baby number three, and that has sort of taken the place of knowing when exactly I can make ‘Princess Diaries 3,’” Hathaway said.

	“I can say I think we had a story breakthrough,” she added. “I think we are moving in the right direction. The script that we were working on — we kind of had to start over with this new direction, which is not the update I think anybody wants, but we all feel really good that this is gonna be the one.”
	

	The continuation of “The Princess Diaries” series has been in production since 2022. Adele Lim (“Crazy Rich Asians,” “Raya and the Last Dragon”) signed on to direct in 2024.

	“We just want to make the story right because it is a franchise that is so beloved by so many people,” Lim told Variety at the Gold Gala carpet in May.

	Hathaway will reprise her role as Mia Thermopolis, now Queen of Genovia, in the threequel. Lim teased that fans of the original movies can expect “a lot of fun returns” from other cast members.

	Lim said in May that the shooting and production schedule were still being worked out, and a release date has yet to be announced.

	“The Princess Diaries” was released in 2001 and was followed by the 2004 sequel, “The Princess Diaries 2: Royal Engagement.” The two films have earned a combined $300 million worldwide.

schema.headline: Anne Hathaway Says ‘Princess Diaries 3’ Had a ‘Story Breakthrough’ After Having to ‘Start Over’ With the Script: ‘We are Moving in the Right Direction’

title: HBO’s ‘Harry Potter’ Cinematographer Says It’s ‘Brutal’ Working Around the Young Stars’ Strict Shooting Schedules: ‘Have to Pre-Shoot All the Scenes While They’re in Class’
description: Adriano Goldman, the cinematographer for HBO’s “Harry Potter” series, said in a recent interview with Andre Pilli that the filming conditions for th
body:
Adriano Goldman, the cinematographer for HBO’s “Harry Potter” series, said in a recent interview with Andre Pilli that the filming conditions for the show have been “brutal” because the camera team must work around the young cast’s strict shooting schedules.

	“You have to shoot really fast, and the faster you go, the more you compromise quality,” Goldman said. “There’s no way around it, right? Obviously, we have a lot of resources, but it’s brutal. The kids’ screen time is really brutal. So the methodology is very specific. You practically have to pre-shoot all the scenes while they’re in class.”
	

	Goldman explained that Dominic McLaughlin (Harry Potter), Alastair Stout (Ron Weasley) and Arabella Stanton (Arabella Stanton) come to set for about “an hour and a half,” and the schedule demands he shoots “six setups” before they have to leave again.


	“It’s really hard; you shoot everything out of order, right, because of that kind of scheduling,” Goldman said. “So the process is really complicated, but I’ve done one now.”

	As noted by Goldman, part of the reason the timelines are so strict is that the child stars have to balance on-set schooling with filming. The BBC reported in July 2025 that Warner Bros. Studios Leavesden, the production home for HBO‘s “Harry Potter,” built a temporary school for the show’s many young actors.

	The Three Rivers District Council permitted the studio to “use a series of portable buildings as a school facility for the next decade. The proposed classrooms have been designed to be used by up to 600 pupils during peak periods, when large crowd scenes are shot, but will typically serve about 150 students. It will operate on weekdays between 5:30 am and 8:30 pm so young actors can fit in their actual studies between night shoots, reshoots and location filming.”
	

	The “Harry Potter” series will debut on HBO and HBO Max on December 25, 2026.

schema.headline: HBO’s ‘Harry Potter’ Cinematographer Says It’s ‘Brutal’ Working Around the Young Stars’ Strict Shooting Schedules: ‘Have to Pre-Shoot All the Scenes While They’re in Class’

title: James Gunn Gave ‘Big Brother 28’ Star Rick Devens a Cameo in ‘Man of Tomorrow’ but He ‘Had to Cancel’
description:
Rick Devens was supposed to have a cameo in James Gunn‘s “Man of Tomorrow,” but the reality star had filming obligations with “Big Brother.”

	Gunn
body:
Rick Devens was supposed to have a cameo in James Gunn‘s “Man of Tomorrow,” but the reality star had filming obligations with “Big Brother.”

	Gunn revealed the cameo after Devens gave the DC Studios co-head a shoutout during the live broadcast of “Big Brother” Season 28’s Thursday eviction episode. Gunn shared the clip on social media, and a follower asked if Devens would appear in the “Superman” sequel.


	“No because he’s on ‘Big Brother’! Otherwise he would have been,” Gunn wrote on Threads.
	

	When asked by another Threads user if  he and Devens know each other, Gunn replied, “We do. He was gonna appear in the background of ‘MoT’ but he had to cancel (his awesome wife and kids still came). He couldn’t tell me why but told me I’d be excited. Obviously I was!”

	“Survivor”-alum Devens was one of three surprise houseguests revealed during the “Big Brother” Season 28 premiere. This season’s cast also features Dee Valladares, Angela Murray, Jason De Puy, Kamuela “Kamu” Kirk, Ashley Trail, Barrett Pfeiffer, Chuk Anyanwu, Drew Campbell, Haley Thogmartin, LaTrice Verrett, Lyric Medeiros, Mallory Aurichio, Melody Morris, Rome Seymour, Taylor Brown and Yash Patel.

	The “Big Brother” contestants compete to win a $750,000 cash prize. Episodes air Wednesdays, Thursdays and Sundays from 8-9 p.m. ET/PT on CBS, and are available to stream on Paramount+ following their on-air debut.

	“Man of Tomorrow” is the sequel to Gunn’s “Superman,” which hit theaters in July 2025. David Corenswet is set to reprise his role as the Man of Steel opposite Nicholas Hoult’s Lex Luthor. The film is slated for a July 9, 2027 release.

schema.headline: James Gunn Gave ‘Big Brother 28’ Star Rick Devens a Cameo in ‘Man of Tomorrow’ but He ‘Had to Cancel’

title: ‘Shark Tank’ Star Kevin O’Leary Sued For Claiming Utah Data Center Opposition Has Ties to China
description: Kevin O’Leary is being sued for defamation by a group fighting data center construction in Utah after the “Shark Tank” star went on Fox News and cla
body:
Kevin O’Leary is being sued for defamation by a group fighting data center construction in Utah after the “Shark Tank” star went on Fox News and claimed some of the individuals involved with the opposition have ties to China, according to The Hill.

	The suit, which was also against Fox News, was filed on Wednesday in Utah federal district court by Alliance for a Better Utah, Elevate Strategies, Gabrielle Finlayson and Josh Kanter. It claimed that Kanter and Finlayson “suffered devastating reputational harm, significant economic losses, severe emotional distress, and ongoing threats to their physical safety” after O’Leary went “on the attack” during his Fox News appearance. 
	

	“Fox was instrumental in O’Leary’s defamatory smear campaign, putting him on air to attack Plaintiffs five times in three weeks,” the suit read. “Despite O’Leary’s inherently unreliable claims, and his clear lack of support for them, Fox repeatedly invited O’Leary onto its programs and allowed him to broadcast his false accusations to millions of viewers without any qualification.”


	Fox News Media said in a statement regarding the lawsuit, “Fox News Media publicly corrected the record on every program where on-air guest Kevin O’Leary’s comments were made, all of which was extensively publicized. We will vigorously defend against this lawsuit.”

	Reps for O’Leary did not immediately respond to a request for comment. 

	In late June, Fox News issued a statement apologizing for O’Leary’s claims. The statement read, “Kevin O’Leary appeared as a guest on the show on May 24 and discussed the ongoing controversy surrounding his planned data center project in Utah. He made certain claims relating to the opponents of his project. Mr. O’Leary has now corrected the record and explained he has ‘no evidence’ that the Alliance for a Better Utah, Josh Kantor or Taylor Knuth are funded by China or the Chinese Communist Party.”
	

	O’Leary issued a statement of his own over social media, walking back the allegations. He wrote, “Recently I appeared on various news programs and would like to clarify that I have no evidence that Alliance for a Better Utah, Elevate Strategies, Gabrielle Finlayson, Taylor Knuth or Josh Kanter are funded by China or the Chinese Communist Party.”

schema.headline: ‘Shark Tank’ Star Kevin O’Leary Sued For Claiming Utah Data Center Opposition Has Ties to China

title: FIFA World Cup Final 2026 Livestream: Here's Where to Watch the Spain vs. Argentina Online for Free
description:
If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission.

	After a m
body:
If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission.

	After a month of gameplay, the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final starts on Sunday, July 19. For the 23rd edition of the international soccer tournament final, the match takes place at New York New Jersey Stadium (MetLife Stadium) in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Kick off is at 3 p.m. ET/12 p.m. PT.

	If you want to catch the final live, the match broadcasts on FOX in English, as well as Telemundo in Spanish. It also livestreams on FOX One and Peacock, respectively.


	The World Cup closing ceremony starts at 1:30 p.m. ET/11:30 a.m. PT before the match begins. It features performances from Nicole Scherzinger, Laura Pausini and Robbie Williams, while it also has influencer IShowSpeed and actor Tom Cruise making special appearances.

	And ahead of Spain vs. Argentina, Jennifer Hudson performs the U.S. national anthem, “The Star-Spangled Banner.”

	Additionally, Madonna, Shakira, BTS and Justin Bieber are scheduled to perform during the World Cup Final Halftime Show with an approximate start time of 4 p.m. ET/1 p.m. PT. Meanwhile, Jennifer Hudson is set to perform “The Star-Spangled Banner” before kickoff.

	The World Cup Final may be a draw to a number of celebrity soccer fans, such as Javier Bardem and Penélope Cruz, Antonio Banderas, Rafael Nadal and others, as well as Timothée Chalamet, Leonardo DiCaprio, Owen Wilson, Tom Holland, Selena Gomez, Drake and more. Tune-in to see which celebrities are attending the final match.

	And with so much star power on and off the pitch, the World Cup Final is expected to be a ratings win for all networks that are broadcasting and live-streaming the match.


	Want to watch FOX, or Telemundo online for free? You can get access to these networks through streaming platforms, like DirecTV, Fubo, Hulu + Live TV, FOX One and others. In fact, some of these streamers offer free trials too.


						
			
	

					DirecTV
	
					

	DirecTV is offering a five-day free trial, which will let you watch FOX, as well as Telemundo for free. There’s an app available that comes with key features, including unlimited DVR storage and up to three devices to stream. In addition, you’ll get access to local and cable channels.

	For FOX and Telemundo, you can sign up for DirecTV’s “Entertainment” Signature Package for $59.99 for the first month of service ($89.99/month afterward) and get more than 90 channels.


						
			
	

					Fox Corp.
	
					

	FOX has its own streaming service called FOX One, which features every network under the FOX umbrella, including FOX News, FOX Weather, FOX Nation, FOX Business, FOX Sports, FS2, Big Ten Network, FOX Deportes, FOX Network and your local FOX station. FOX One starts at $19.99/month ($199.99/year), or you can signup with the service as a channel add-on for Prime Video. In fact, go with Prime Video and get a three-day free trial just for signing up.

	Please note: You have to be a subscriber to Prime Video to get FOX One as an add-on channel.

	Sign up for a 30-day free trial to take advantage of all that Amazon Prime has to offer, including access to Prime Video, Prime Gaming and Amazon Photos; fast free shipping in less than two days with Prime Delivery; in-store discounts at Whole Foods Market; access to exclusive shopping deals and events — such as Black Friday in November — and much more. Learn about Amazon Prime and its benefits here.


						
			
	

					NBCUniversal
	
					

	The World Cup Final livestream on Telemundo is accessible on Peacock, so the best way to watch the tournament in Spanish is to get a subscription to the streaming services at $10.99/month.

	Not a subscriber? You can get a Peacock monthly subscription to stream the World Cup Final online, and keep it to watch other hit movies and TV shows — including “Wicked: For Good,” “Hamnet,” “Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair,” “Oppenheimer,” “The Traitors,” “Vanderpump Rules” and others.

	Luckily, the 23rd edition of the World Cup Final is not a pay-per-view event, so there is no PPV price needed. You can watch the Spanish-language broadcast online simply with a Peacock subscription.



						
			
	

					Sling TV
	
					

	Sling is one of the best options for affordable live streaming with the Sling Blue package, which goes for $45.99/month. The package includes FOX. World Cup fans can broadcast the match in Spanish on Telemundo, which is featured in the Sling Latino package for just $10/month.

	Please note: Pricing and channel availability are subject to your local TV market.


						
			
	

					Fubo
	
					

	Fubo offers a five-day free trial for new users who sign up, and the streaming service includes access to FOX, as well as Telemundo. The service offers a promo that’ll get you up to $30 off the first month at $63.99 (reg. $88.99/month) for the Fubo Pro plan. Once set up, customers get access to the FOX One streaming service (via Fubo login credentials) and more than 220 live TV channels too.


						
			
	

					Hulu
	
					

	Hulu + Live TV, which has a three-day free trial, gives you access to FOX and Telemundo, along with more than 100 live TV channels for $89.99/month. The streaming service is bundled with an expanded content library with Disney+, Hulu and ESPN Unlimited at no additional cost.

schema.headline: FIFA World Cup Final 2026 Livestream: Here's Where to Watch the Spain vs. Argentina Online for Free

title: ‘The Odyssey’ Becomes Christopher Nolan’s Biggest Global Debut of All Time With $264 Million
description:
“The Odyssey” is off to an epic start at the box office.

	Universal’s action-adventure generated $264.1 million globally over the weekend, includin
body:
“The Odyssey” is off to an epic start at the box office.

	Universal’s action-adventure generated $264.1 million globally over the weekend, including $139.6 million internationally and $124.5 million domestically. It’s a towering debut for a movie that’s R-rated, nearly three hours in length and isn’t part of a major franchise. Of course, “The Odyssey” isn’t original — it’s adapted from Homer’s roughly 3,000-year-old epic poem — but the film’s initial success is a testament to Nolan’s outsized popularity as a filmmaker. He’s long been a box office draw, having directed blockbusters like “The Dark Knight” trilogy, “Inception,” “Dunkirk” and “Interstellar.” But Nolan reached new levels of acclaim after 2023’s “Oppenheimer” became a box office behemoth and won the Oscar for best picture.
	

	“The Odyssey” marks the biggest worldwide opening of Nolan’s career, ahead of 2012’s The Dark Knight Rises” with $249 million and 2009’s “The Dark Knight” with $198 million. (This benchmark comes with a caveat because ticket sales weren’t adjusted for inflation.) It’s also the third-biggest opening of the year behind “The Super Mario Galaxy Movie” ($372.5 million) and “Toy Story 5” ($312 million).


	Nolan wrote and directed “The Odyssey,” which stars Matt Damon as Odysseus, the king of Ithaca, who endures a treacherous journey to get home after the Trojan War. Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, Robert Pattinson, Lupita Nyong’o, Zendaya, Charlize Theron, Elliot Page, Jon Bernthal and John Leguizamo round out the starry ensemble. With stellar reviews and great word-of-mouth, “The Odyssey” is primed for a long, long run in theaters.
	

	“The Odyssey” opened in 73 international markets, with top territories including the United Kingdom and Ireland with $17.4 million, France with $13.7 million and Italy with $11.5 million. “The Odyssey” stands as Nolan’s biggest opening in 48 of those territories, including France and Italy, as well as Universal’s largest debut in 11 markets. The film has yet to open in South Korea, China or Japan.

	“With ‘The Odyssey,’ we had the extraordinary opportunity to unite moviegoers worldwide around a story that has resonated across cultures for centuries. Christopher Nolan‘s passion, vision and unique cinematic voice consistently drive anticipation and attendance on a truly global scale. He, alongside the force that is Emma Thomas, continues to set the standard for ambitious, event filmmaking,” said Veronika Kwan Vandenberg, president of distribution for Universal Pictures International. “‘The Odyssey’ is that rare kind of movie that brings audiences together across every market in a shared cultural moment.”

	“The Odyssey” is notable as the first feature shot entirely with Imax cameras, and those premium large formats contributed a sizable portion of initial ticket sales. The movie earned $22.2 million internationally (16% of revenues) and $51.8 million globally (23% of revenues) in Imax, delivering the company’s biggest opening in like-for-like markets ahead of “Avengers: Endgame.” And it’s currently pacing 47% ahead of “Oppenheimer,” which was particularly huge in Imax with $35 million worldwide to start and $180 million by the end of its run.

	“If you don’t see ‘The Odyssey’ in Imax, you aren’t seeing it in the way that Chris Nolan intended,” says the company’s CEO Richard Gelfond. He called this weekend a “transcendent moment” for Imax, predicting the ripple effect from “The Odyssey” will lead to “more Imax theaters opening and more audiences seeking out” the format.

schema.headline: ‘The Odyssey’ Becomes Christopher Nolan’s Biggest Global Debut of All Time With $264 Million

title: Tom Cruise and Half of Hollywood Light Up the 2026 World Cup Final
description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has smashed ratings records in the U.S. and around the world, owing to its unique mix of star power — Lionel Messi, Cristian
body:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup has smashed ratings records in the U.S. and around the world, owing to its unique mix of star power — Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and Neymar performing in perhaps their final World Cup matches, joined by younger stars Kylian Mbappe, Erling Haaland and Lamine Yamal — a North American setting and a bevy of certified studs. And speaking of star power, Tom Cruise, one of Hollywood’s last movie stars, has an Oscar-courting movie coming out in the fall, “Digger,” that he’s been promoting the heck out of. So naturally, the two would merge their audiences, first with “Digger” airing a number of special TV spots during World Cup matches, and then Cruise making a special appearance during Sunday’s World Cup final between Spain and defending champions Argentina at New Jersey’s MetLife Stadium (temporarily renamed the New York New Jersey Stadium just for the World Cup).
	


	After hanging with “Sesame Street’s” Elmo and Cookie Monster prior to the match, the “Mission: Impossible” and “Top Gun” star delivered an inspirational speech opening the World Cup final, where he proclaimed, “So, as we gather for one final chapter, let us celebrate a tournament that brought the world together. Let us celebrate each other. This is football. This is unity. This is greatness.” A number of other celebrities were in attendance at the final, including President Donald Trump (who was showered with boos), “The Odyssey” star Matt Damon and his wife, Timothée Chalamet and his girlfriend Kylie Jenner, Mick Jagger, Justin Bieber, Will Ferrell, Victor Wembanyama, Post Malone, Pharell Williams (who announcers misidentified as A$AP Rocky), Trevor Noah, Anya Taylor-Joy (in an Argentina jersey, since she’s part-Argentinian), two-time Oscar winner Adrien Brody and more.
	

	And then came the halftime show.

Tom Cruise gives an inspirational speech while opening the FIFA #WorldCup final: "So as we gather for one final chapter, let us celebrate a tournament that brought the world together. Let us celebrate each other. This is football. This is unity. This is greatness."(via FOX) pic.twitter.com/xNxQPhnaqA— Variety (@Variety) July 19, 2026


	Curated by Coldplay’s Chris Martin, the World Cup’s first-ever official halftime show was co-headlined by BTS, Justin Bieber, Madonna and Shakira, highlighting the competition’s mostly American backdrop and its international scope. With the match scoreless at the half, the crowds in attendance and watching on TV needed an adrenaline boost, and the medley of musical acts was more than eager to provide one.

	Oh, and there was Jason Sudeikis, who popped out as his Ted Lasso character to introduce Justin Bieber, urging him to “make us proud” while posing in front of a “BELIEBE” sign.


	To say that the event was star-studded would be a vast understatement. The 2026 FIFA World Cup final was the hottest ticket since Game 4 of the NBA Finals, attracting celebrities from all over the world to… New Jersey.

schema.headline: Tom Cruise and Half of Hollywood Light Up the 2026 World Cup Final

title: Box Office: ‘The Odyssey’ Stuns With $124 Million Opening Weekend, Third-Biggest Debut of the Year
description: It’s a box office debut fit for a king. Christopher Nolan‘s action epic “The Odyssey” ignited to $124.5 million from 3,919 domestic theaters over th
body:
It’s a box office debut fit for a king. Christopher Nolan‘s action epic “The Odyssey” ignited to $124.5 million from 3,919 domestic theaters over the weekend, defying expectations and setting up the R-rated spectacle for a long, long journey in theaters. 

	These ticket sales are notable as Nolan’s biggest debut since 2012’s “The Dark Knight Rises” ($160 million) as well as the third-largest opening of the year following “Toy Story 5” ($159 million) and “The Super Mario Galaxy Movie” ($131 million). They’re also impressive not just because “The Odyssey” is a nearly three-hour sword-and-sandal adventure, a genre that’s rarely made these days, but also because it is rated R, which limits who can buy tickets. 
	

	At the international box office, “The Odyssey” collected $139.6 million from 73 markets for a stellar worldwide tally of $264.1 million. This marks Nolan’s biggest global opening ahead of “The Dark Knight Rises” ($249 million, not adjusted for inflation), 2009’s “The Dark Knight” ($198 million, not adjusted for inflation) and “Oppenheimer” ($180 million). 


	Adapted from Homer’s 3,000-year-old epic poem, “The Odyssey” stars Matt Damon as Odysseus, the king of Ithaca, and chronicles his seemingly eternal, perilous voyage home after the Trojan War. Damon leads the all-star ensemble of Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, Robert Pattinson, Lupita Nyong’o, Zendaya, Charlize Theron, Elliot Page, Jon Bernthal and John Leguizamo. Written and directed by Nolan, “The Odyssey” is one of the best-reviewed films of the year, with an “A” grade on CinemaScore exit polls and 95% from critics on Rotten Tomatoes. Males accounted for 56% of ticket buyers, while half of audience members were between the ages of 18 and 34, underscoring that Nolan appeals to all generations of moviegoers.
	

	With stellar word-of-mouth, the frenzy around premium formats and an A-list cast that’s been taking the promotional circuit by storm, “The Odyssey” is primed for an epic box office run. Plus, it won’t have any competition until Sony’s “Spider-Man: Brand New Day” opens on July 31. That’s necessary to justify the film’s massive price tag. Universal spent $250 million to produce — a staggering cost for any tentpole these days, especially one that’s rated R — and another $125 million to market to global audiences. 

	“Christopher Nolan is the only filmmaker working today who could take an ancient story like this, attract a cast as sprawling and accomplished as this, and make it a pop culture event,” says David A. Gross, who publishes the box office newsletter FranchiseRe. “Nolan has become a unique and singular talent, with a loyal and enthusiastic following.”

	Nolan has long been a commercial draw, having directed outsized box office triumphs like “The Dark Knight” trilogy, “Inception,” “Dunkirk” and “Interstellar.” But the British filmmaker reached a new level of fame after 2023’s “Oppenheimer,” which became a box office juggernaut before securing the Oscar for best picture. Fueled by the “Barbenheimer” phenomenon, “Oppenheimer” launched above expectations to $82 million and earned a remarkable $330 million in North America and $975 million globally by the end of its run. While “Oppenheimer” opened in second place behind “Barbie,” another box office behemoth, “The Odyssey” gets the satisfaction of debuting at No. 1 across the globe.

	“There’s a palpable excitement in theaters this weekend,” says Universal’s domestic distribution president Jim Orr. “Christopher Nolan is one of the most trusted filmmakers ever, and audiences know he’s going to deliver something that you want to experience on the big screen. We have showtimes in certain formats that are sold out for next weekend, the weekend after, and even mid-week.”

	Nolan has become synonymous with premium large formats, and “The Odyssey” is notable as the first feature shot entirely with Imax cameras. It’s a feat that Nolan has called his “longest-held ambition.” Audiences have taken note of the hype around these screens. Premium large formats contributed a massive 53% of the film’s domestic tally, with Imax accounting for $51.8 million or 23% of global ticket sales. Imax, which is dedicating its entire global footprint to “The Odyssey” for three weeks, has been scheduling showtimes around the clock in the coveted 70mm auditoriums, with some determined cinephiles enduring 2 a.m. and 7 a.m. screenings of the nearly three-hour epic. 
	

	“’The Odyssey’ is among a rarified group of instant classic blockbusters in Imax history that fully leverage our platform; as bullish as we are on its success, our experience tells us that even we don’t know how this movie will grow our brand and business,” says Rich Gelfond, CEO of Imax. “With record pre-sales, critical and audience acclaim, and the way the film presents extraordinarily in Imax, we believe all the elements are in place for ‘The Odyssey’ to enjoy a very long and successful run.”

	Thanks to “The Odyssey,” the box office is rebounding after three consecutive duds: the Warner Bros. and DC adaptation “Supergirl,” Universal and Illumination sequel “Minions and Monsters” and Disney’s live action “Moana.” Despite those stumbles, domestic ticket sales are roughly 10% above 2025, according to Rentrak, and the summer season is expected to surpass $4 billion for the second time since the pandemic upended the industry.

	“Nolan’s latest epic has arrived at the perfect time, reminding everyone of the undeniable allure of the big-screen moviegoing experience,” says Paul Dergarabedian, Rentrak’s head of marketplace trends.

	“Moana” landed in a very distant second place with $19 million in its sophomore frame — a 56% decline from its disappointing $43 million debut. So far, Disney’s live-action remake has generated $82 million domestically and $178 million globally. Since “Moana” was so expensive (it carries a $250 million price tag) and theater owners get to keep roughly half of revenues, the film is primed to lose at least $100 million in its theatrical run. Disney’s summer has been mixed, with the studio fielding outsized hits like “The Devil Wears Prada 2” ($688 million) and “Toy Story 5” along with big-budget misfires like “Moana” and “Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu” ($340 million against a $165 million budget).

	“Minions and Monsters” tied with “Toy Story 5” for third place, with each film bringing in $14.8 million over the weekend. 

	For “Minions and Monsters,” those ticket sales represent a 34% drop from the prior weekend and boost revenues to $137 million domestically and $323 million globally. Since prior entries in Universal and Illumination’s “Despicable Me” franchise have reached or surpassed the billion-dollar mark with ease, the newest “Minions” may wind up as the lowest-grossing of the bunch. However, this installment was produced for $85 million and will turn a profit for the studio.
	

	“Toy Story 5” is one summer’s biggest hits with $430 million in North America and $958 million worldwide after five weekends of release. It’ll soon surpass $1 billion and eventually overtake its predecessor, 2019’s “Toy Story 4” ($1.07 billion), as the highest-grossing entry in the beloved Pixar franchise.

	Meanwhile, “Supergirl” dropped to No. 9 with $1.5 million in its fourth weekend. After a month in theaters, the latest comic book adaptation from Warner Bros. and DC is fizzling with $69.9 million domestically and $120 million globally. Given its hefty $170 million budget, “Supergirl” is projected to lose more than $120 million in its theatrical run. It’s the second notable flop in 2026 for Warner Bros., following this spring’s “The Bride!” which flatlined with $23 million worldwide against a $90 million budget.

	Elsewhere, A24’s comedy “The Invite” has collected $3.9 million over the weekend from 2,110 screens, its widest footprint to date. After about a month in limited release, Olivia Wilde’s movie has grossed a promising $15 million.

schema.headline: Box Office: ‘The Odyssey’ Stuns With $124 Million Opening Weekend, Third-Biggest Debut of the Year

title: 13 Best Performances in Christopher Nolan Movies: From Matt Damon to Heath Ledger
description:
Jul 19, 2026 10:00am PT	
	

	
		13 Best Performances in Christopher Nolan Movies: From Matt Damon to Heath Ledger
body:
Jul 19, 2026 10:00am PT	
	

	
		13 Best Performances in Christopher Nolan Movies: From Matt Damon to Heath Ledger
	
	
			


			
			
	

									Warner Bros. / Universal Pictures / Paramount Pictures
	

		
	
				

	Time has been kind to Christopher Nolan. 

	The bending cities, rotating hallways, ticking clocks and practical explosions he captures on film have made him one of the most popular and vital directors working today. His newest venture, “The Odyssey,” arrives in theaters with Matt Damon stepping into the armor of Homer’s wandering king, and it offers a chance to examine the quieter through-line of Nolan’s career: his ability to draw unforgettable performances from actors.

	For all his technical ambition and reputation for precision, Nolan has become one of the most reliable actors’ directors of his generation. His films produced two acting Oscar winners in a single night, with Cillian Murphy and Robert Downey Jr. triumphing for the best picture winner “Oppenheimer.” Years earlier, Heath Ledger delivered one of the most celebrated posthumous performances in modern film history in “The Dark Knight.”

	But Nolan has also elevated movie stars and transformed character actors who never landed major accolades. You can still hear the cries from Nolan fans who felt performances such as Stephen Tobolowsky’s devastating Sammy in “Memento” (2000) were snubbed entirely, or that Aaron Eckhart deserved a nod alongside his co-star Ledger for his portrayal of Harvey Dent, aka Two-Face, in “The Dark Knight.”

	Nonetheless, the director repeatedly gravitates toward actors who can hold a viewer’s attention with a single glance, performers capable of suggesting an internal catastrophe while a literal one unfolds in the background.

	Below, Variety ranks the 13 best performances in Christopher Nolan’s films.

	Honorable mentions: Emily Blunt (“Oppenheimer”); Leonardo DiCaprio (“Inception”); Joseph Gordon-Levitt (“Inception”); Carrie-Anne Moss (“Memento”); Robert Pattinson (“The Odyssey”); Robert Pattinson (“Tenet”); Jeremy Theobald (“Following”)


			
			
	John Leguizamo (‘The Odyssey’)
			
										
					Image Credit: ©Universal/Courtesy Everett Collection				
					
				

	Despite an illustrious career spanning his acclaimed solo TV special “Freak” and memorable film roles in classics like “Carlito’s Way” (1993) and “Romeo + Juliet” (1996), Academy recognition has eluded Latino veteran multi-hyphenate John Leguizamo.

	Beautifully compelling, his work as Eumaeus, Odysseus’ faithful and blind swineherd in “The Odyssey,” is a perfect balance of heart, peppered throughout the intricate parts of a movie so dense with violence and disturbing imagery. Even the way you hear the cry in his voice as he unknowingly speaks to Odysseus as he is battered and bruised, lying in his bed, is one of the defining moments of the sprawling 3-hour epic.

		
			
			
	Al Pacino (‘Insomnia’)
			
										
					Image Credit: ©Warner Bros/Courtesy Everett Collection				
					
				

	This is a quieter Pacino performance, stripped of his usual theatrical intensity, as he portrays Detective Will Dormer, a Los Angeles investigator chasing a killer through Alaska’s endless daylight. Pacino plays a man whose exhaustion is on full display, and whose guilt, sleeplessness and compromised choices slowly consume him.

	In addition, his co-star Robin Williams delivers a chilling counterpoint as the killer, but the film belongs to Pacino’s unraveling.

	It was Nolan’s first major studio feature and an early indication that he could operate within Hollywood’s machinery without losing the human damage at the center of his stories.

		
			
			
	Robert Downey Jr. (‘Oppenheimer’)
			
										
					Image Credit: ©Universal/Courtesy Everett Collection				
					
				

	After years as one of Hollywood’s biggest movie stars, Nolan directed Robert Downey Jr. to one of his most disciplined performances.

	As Lewis Strauss, the government official whose wounded pride fuels a campaign against the father of the atomic bomb in “Oppenheimer,” Downey avoids obvious, simplistic villainy. Instead, he builds Strauss slowly, with charm, intelligence and resentment as it unspools over the dynamic biopic.

	It’s the role that brought Downey to the stage as the winner for best supporting actor and marked a moment of a remarkable second act for a star that has risen like a phoenix, into the suit of “Iron Man” and in the clothes of Charlie Chaplin, and still can surprise us all.

		
			
			
	Christian Bale (‘Batman Begins’)
			
										
					Image Credit: Everett Collection				
					
				

	For many fans, Christian Bale is (their) Batman.

	The British actor had already established himself as one of his generation’s most fearless performers, but taking over the cape and cowl after Michael Keaton, Val Kilmer and George Clooney was no small task. Bale made the role his own, grounding Bruce Wayne with depth and transforming him into a believable symbol of fear and justice.

	While many consider “The Dark Knight” (2008) the crown jewel of Nolan’s trilogy, Bale’s finest performance as the character comes in “Batman Begins” (2005). Watching Bruce Wayne evolve from a traumatized heir into Gotham’s protector gives Bale the most complete emotional arc of the three films.

	The trilogy also cemented Bale’s status as one of Hollywood’s premier leading men. He won the Academy Award for best supporting actor for “The Fighter” (2010) and has earned three additional Oscar nominations since.

		
			
			
	Mark Rylance (‘Dunkirk’)
			
										
					Image Credit: Warner Bros.				
					
				

	In a movie with little dialogue and fragmented perspectives, Academy Award winner Mark Rylance (“Bridge of Spies”) makes his quiet heroism feel gargantuan in Nolan’s 2017 best picture nominee “Dunkirk.”

	As Mr. Dawson, a civilian who ventures toward the chaos of the war-torn Dunkirk, it’s Rylance’s nuance, courage and strength communicated through his eyes that is most effective, with his thoughtful glances and perfectly timed pauses. The added layer of personal grief that is hidden beneath the surface, and only revealed through fleeting moments, is where the beloved thespian shows his expert skills.

		
			
			
	Matthew McConaughey (‘Interstellar’)
			
										
					Image Credit: Paramount Pictures				
					
				

	Do you remember where you were the first time you watched Cooper watch decades of messages from his children?

	If you were lucky to see it in a movie theater, you could have found yourself fighting for breath and not to sob loudly because Matthew McConaughey, who was coming off his Oscar-winning role in “Dallas Buyers Club” the year prior, is the emotional centerpiece of Nolan’s space drama “Interstellar.”

	Grounded in Nolan’s cosmic ambitions, McConaughey steers the audience through galaxies, wormholes, and floating bookshelves, building something unforgettable rooted in the themes of regret and the unique bond between fathers and daughters.

		
			
			
	Christian Bale (‘The Prestige’)
			
										
					Image Credit: ©Touchstone Pictures/Courtesy Everett Collection				
					
				

	Bale is perhaps one of Nolan’s greatest chameleons, and “The Prestige” is one of his most intricate assignments.

	As Alfred Borden, a magician locked in a devastating rivalry with Hugh Jackman’s Robert Angier, Bale showcases an obsession that is disguised as merely dedication. To explain more of his essential choices only risks diminishing the film’s carefully constructed deception (especially for those who haven’t had the pleasure of watching the film yet). However, it’s fair to say that Bale’s performance is itself an illusion and he creates a man whose entire identity is built around concealment.

	It may be the quietest transformation of Bale’s Nolan collaborations.

		
			
			
	Marion Cotillard (‘Inception’)
			
								
				

	Academy Award winner Marion Cotillard (“La Vie en Rose”) has been a “North Star” for me when it comes to how Nolan should write and cast his leading ladies. 

	As Mal, the deceased wife who haunts Leonardo DiCaprio’s dream thief, Cotillard turns his memory into a weapon. As she seduces and terrifies, she also delivers heartbreak, manifesting it into grief that threatens to collapse the entire mind.

	She transforms Nolan’s most cerebral blockbuster into something unexpectedly intimate, and by my accounts, was worthy of an Oscar bid that year (that also came with standout turns in “Public Enemies” and “Nine”).

		
			
			
	Matt Damon (‘The Odyssey’)
			
										
					Image Credit: ©Universal/Courtesy Everett Collection				
					
				

	In “The Odyssey,” Nolan reunites with Matt Damon, his “Interstellar” and “Oppenheimer” supporting player, giving him the most mythical challenge of his career and paying off in dividends.

	As Odysseus, Damon is tasked with carrying the sprawling epic about a warrior king on his quest to return home. The role requires physical endurance, in addition to emotional erosion. Damon achieves just that.

	The question of whether Odysseus belongs higher on this list will unfold with the passage of time. However, it is already evident that Nolan has placed his trust in Damon as the anchor of his latest endeavor, and he will likely be remembered for years to come.

		
			
			
	Cillian Murphy (‘Oppenheimer’)
			
										
					Image Credit: Universal Pictures				
					
				

	For years, Murphy has existed as Nolan’s secret weapon. Constantly being a performer whose intensity could be felt even when he occupied only the edges of a frame.

	But with the 2024 biopic blockbuster “Oppenheimer,” Nolan put him front and center, with the Irish star delivering one of the defining performances of the decade as J. Robert Oppenheimer, the father of the atomic bomb.

	In multiple scenes, Nolan chooses to fixate on Murphy’s face, up close and personal — showcasing the ambition, arrogance and curiosity that turn the scientist’s stillness into fully realized drama. Murphy won the Oscar for best actor, becoming the first Irishman to win the category, and completing one of the most rewarding actor-director collaborations of Nolan’s career.

		
			
			
	Samantha Morton (‘The Odyssey’)
			
										
					Image Credit: Universal Pictures				
					
				

	Samantha Morton is that girl.

	The English two-time Oscar-nominated actress for “Sweet and Lowdown” (1999) and “In America” (2003) delivers perhaps the single best performance of her career with only about 10 minutes of screen time in Nolan’s mythological epic “The Odyssey.”

	Stealing the movie as Circe, the witch of the island of Aeaea, who appears midway through the film, is a captivating presence, harnessing the power and focus of the film’s mythological aura. Cinephiles and critics have often criticized Nolan’s portrayal of female characters (often, rightfully so). However, he nails the evolution and almost dancelike quality in which Morton casts her spell on Odysseus’ soldiers, and then again, with their return. 

	It potentially could be an Oscar-winning portrayal.

		
			
			
	Guy Pearce (‘Memento’)
			
								
				

	Before Nolan became a blockbuster institution, many cinephiles learned about the auteur and his capabilities as someone willing to take enormous creative risks, as shown by his audacious 2000 film “Memento,” headlined by the riveting Guy Pearce.

	While Nolan and his co-writer and brother Jonathan received Oscar bids for original screenplay (along with one of the most deserved film editing noms of the decade for Dody Dorn), Pearce was passed over by every major awards group that year. A criminal overlook.

	As Leonard Shelby, who is unable to form new memories, Pearce transforms a character that could have been a high-concept gimmick in lesser hands, and instead creates a devastating portrait of grief. He also gives the viewer a nearly impossible task — how do we trust a man who cannot trust himself?

		
			
			
	Heath Ledger (‘The Dark Knight’)
			
								
				

	Chaos in human form, as well as brilliance. 

	This positioning may be anticlimactic for some, especially if you read Variety‘s 62 best superhero movie performances from 2023, which Heath Ledger topped. Nonetheless, it’s warranted again here.

	Ledger’s lip-smacking anarchist and arch-nemesis of the Caped Crusader captivated audiences in Nolan’s 2008 superhero sequel “The Dark Knight.” Unfortunately, his untimely death came before the film’s release, leaving him unable to see the magnitude his presence had and the acclaim he would receive. He won the Oscar for best supporting actor, becoming only the second performer to receive a posthumous honor from the Academy. 

	Many comic book villains have been chasing this type of performance ever since, and none have been able to match it.

		
	
	


	
		More from Variety
	
	
					

			
			
			
			
	
			
					

			
			
			
			
	
			
					

			
			
			
			
	
			
					

			
			
			
			
	
			
					

			
			
			
			
	
			
					

			
			
			
			
	
			
			
	
			
		

		

	
		More From Our Brands
	
	
		

			
	
			
			

	Rolling Stone

					Post Malone Debuts New Song ‘Chrome Heartbreaker’ at World Cup Final Pregame Ceremony		

					
				
			
			
	
			
	
		
			
	
			
			

	Robb Report

					Oregon Is Becoming a World-Class Sparkling Wine Region		

					
				
			
			
	
			
	
		
			
	
			
			

	Sportico

					Kerr of the Moment: Record Mile Attempt Is Months in Making		

					
				
			
			
	
			
	
		
			
	
			
			

	WWD

					Jennifer Hudson Goes Full Thom Browne Americana for the World Cup Final Anthem

title: 'RHOC' Alum Jeana Keough Diagnosed With Tongue Cancer
description:
'RHOC' Alum Jeana Keough
                          Announces Tongue Cancer Diagnosis
body:
'RHOC' Alum Jeana Keough
                          Announces Tongue Cancer Diagnosis
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 19, 2026
                            1:10 PM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Jeana Keough is facing the fight of her life ... the former 'Real Housewives of Orange County' star has been diagnosed with tongue cancer.
      
    
            The heartbreaking news was shared by her daughter, Kara Keough Bosworth, who revealed Jeana initially thought a painful sore on her tongue was caused by a tooth rubbing against it. But after months of discomfort, a biopsy confirmed she has squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Kara says Jeana has already undergone two surgeries one to remove the tumor and another to remove lymph nodes but doctors later discovered the cancer had spread. She's now in her fifth week of chemotherapy, radiation, and immunotherapy.
      

            According to Kara, the treatments have left her mom exhausted and struggling to eat or drink because of severe pain in her mouth and throat. She's also been unable to work her commission-based real estate job while undergoing treatment, creating additional financial strain.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            To help offset medical and living expenses, Kara launched a GoFundMe for Jeana, saying she and her brothers want to make sure their mom feels supported throughout her cancer battle.
      
    
            Jeana was one of the original cast members on Bravo's "The Real Housewives of Orange County," appearing on the series for five seasons before stepping away from life as a full-time Housewife.

title: Peru Earthquake Kills at Least 5, Leaves Hundreds Displaced
description:
Peru Earthquake
                          At Least 5 Dead & Hundreds Displaced
body:
Peru Earthquake
                          At Least 5 Dead & Hundreds Displaced
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 19, 2026
                            8:50 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            A powerful earthquake tore through Peru's Andes region over the weekend ... leaving at least five people dead, more than 20 injured and hundreds forced from their homes, authorities say.
      
    
            Officials say the 5.5-magnitude quake struck Saturday night near the city of Sicaya in central Peru at a shallow depth of about 6 miles, triggering widespread damage across the region.
      
    
      
      #TemblorInternacionales 🇵🇪, un fuerte temblor de 5.5 ocurrió en Perú con epicentro al sur de Chupaca, el sismo causó varios daños en la ciudad de Huancayo, capital del departamento de Junín pic.twitter.com/s8Zrgv16Ry
              @wenewsec
          
  
    
            Authorities say several buildings partially collapsed, including a church and convent, while homes in some of the hardest-hit farming communities were reduced to rubble. Images from local TV showed residents huddled outside overnight as emergency crews searched damaged neighborhoods.
      

      
      Autoridades de #Perú informaron que el #Sismo de magnitud 5.1 con epicentro a 7 kilómetros al sur de Chupaca en la región Junín, provocó una intensidad IV-V en la zona y dejó varios puntos del distrito de Chilca en Huancayo sin energía eléctrica. Medios locales confirmaron la… pic.twitter.com/gi7fcqXTuz
              @daviddelapaz
  
    
            Peru's National Civil Defense Institute says at least 300 people have been displaced, and officials are still working to determine how many people remain missing.
      
    
            The country sits along the Pacific "Ring of Fire," one of the world's most seismically active regions, making earthquakes a frequent threat. In 2007, a devastating 7.9-magnitude quake killed nearly 600 people in southern Peru.

title: Anne Hathaway Then & Now -- Good Genes or Good Docs, Hot Shots
description:
Anne Hathaway Then & Now
                          Good Genes or Good Docs?!
body:
Anne Hathaway Then & Now
                          Good Genes or Good Docs?!
                          Hot Shots 🔥
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 19, 2026
                            12:01 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Anne Hathaway's looks over the years are "Prada"-approved from head to toe!
      
    
            Here is an 18-year-old version of the young Hollywood actress showing off her bedazzled décolletage at "The Princess Diaries" Premiere back in 2001 (left).
      
    
            And, 25 years later, Anne was glowing at the "The Odyssey" photocall earlier this month in Paris (right).
      
    
            We know she serves elevated style and gives us just enough of that killer silhouette -- and leaves us wanting more -- but the question here is:
      
    
            Good Genes or Good Docs?!
